_comment: Isogon Ventures 'Investor' cut. Isogon invests at Series A in the 'technological backbone of the real economy' — advanced software, automation, and AI infrastructure for manufacturing, logistics, energy, and physical systems. This is Adam's EXACT hard-tech / U.S.-reindustrialization thesis, so — unlike the 16VC/M12 cuts — this variant LEADS WITH the thesis (the Toyota/Booz Allen pattern): the ~100 industrial founder/operator interviews, SBIR/STTR fluency, and Aeon's oil-&-gas + manufacturing engagements (technical credibility with engineers/COOs), then Atento $100M+ sourcing/diligence, then 0->1 operating + fund ops. JD core: sourcing, thesis development, commercial + technical diligence, founder relationships, investment memos, firm-building; required 3-8 yrs VC/GE/IB OR operating roles at tech/industrial companies. Built by /tailor-resume. Never fabricate.
name: Adam Breaux
summary: Investor-operator focused on the technological backbone of the real economy — AI, automation, and advanced software reaching manufacturing, energy, and industrial systems. Built a hard-tech / U.S.-reindustrialization thesis from the ground up through ~100 founder and operator interviews across energy, manufacturing, and industrials, and now co-builds bespoke AI products for upstream oil & gas and manufacturers — engaging COOs and field engineers credibly on technical problems. Three years across the venture lifecycle: sourcing and diligencing deals for a $100M+ foundation VC arm (sourced more finalists than any other fellow; 3-for-3 on validated pass calls), building B2B companies from 0 to 1, and running fund operations on an $8M fund. Fluent in SBIR/STTR non-dilutive funding and rare frontier-AI tooling ('AdamGPT'). Operates like an owner.

items.title: Co-Founder
items.org: Aeon  —  bespoke AI products for industrial companies
bullets: Co-founded a practice building bespoke AI products for mid-market energy and manufacturing companies; own executive discovery, problem scoping, technical solutioning, and adoption — sitting with COOs and field engineers to find the bottleneck and ship the fix.

Flagship engagement with a regionally top-tier upstream oil & gas operator (nine-figure budget): built a system that ingests every drilling-report format and auto-generates their morning reporting (BHA analysis, depth-vs-days curves, field callouts), plus a tool that designs a new-well strategy from historical offset-well data — compressing a weeks-long analysis into minutes.
Primary-source view of where AI and automation actually create durable value in physical industries — and where they don't — built from being in the field, not reading about it.

items.title: Associate — Chief of Staff to CEO / Venture Operations Lead
bullets: 0-to-1 venture lead: Took Agentech from a blank page to a funded company (~$25M valuation today) — thesis through pivot to AI-powered insurance-claims servicing; built the team, coordinated engineering/product/design/marketing, and ran sales to the first $600K ARR. Now its part-time CFO and Chief of Staff. Built four companies from 0 to 1 across the studio.

Diligence & financial modeling: Built three-statement, returns, and unit-economics models and ran competitive analysis across 500+ companies; computed fund TVPI for fundraising. Owned Agentech's $3M seed end to end — investor memo, financial model, projections, and data room — to a fast, fully-subscribed close, and ran the same process across multiple ventures (~$10M raised studio-wide).

Fund operations (built from scratch): For an $8M fund, defined the metrics that mattered, stood up portfolio tracking, reporting, and portfolio-review presentations, became the direct point of contact LPs come to for updates, and led the annual LP meeting end to end.

Production AI systems: Built a multi-agent sourcing engine with a continual-learning loop (ranked by geography, thesis, and stage; enriched via Clay) that produced a pipeline of hundreds of targets, plus a Claude-based operations platform spanning CRM, call transcripts, email, calendar, and contracts.

items.title: Venture Fellow / Investment Diligence
items.org: Atento Capital  —  VC arm of the George Kaiser Family Foundation ($100M+ fund)
bullets: Sourced 14 of 20 finalists and 4 of 7 startups selected to pitch — the most of any of ~12 fellows; screened hundreds of companies and built relationships with founders across the pipeline.

Ran screening calls, diligenced founding teams, assessed business models and commercial viability, and presented investment theses to internal committees; recalled by principals to support diligence on two Core Fund I deals (bottom-up market sizing, competitive analysis, financial-projection review) delivered directly to a GP.
Judgment, validated: On all three deals I diligenced, I recommended against — on scalability, market size, and an unconvincing expansion story; all three are no longer operating.

items.title: Hard-tech / U.S.-reindustrialization thesis — built from primary research
bullets: Conducted ~100 founder and operator interviews across energy, manufacturing, and industrials to map where technology and industry shifts are creating the conditions for important new companies — the core conviction: durable advantage is migrating to the harder, physical end of the stack (robotics, automation, energy, industrial infrastructure) as application software commoditizes.

Fluent in SBIR/STTR non-dilutive funding; engaged with OCAST and Oklahoma Catalyst, which administer those programs — a working knowledge of how deep-tech founders actually fund the early, capital-intensive years.

items.title: Founding President — starTUp (University of Tulsa VC Club)
bullets: Founded TU's first VC club and placed members as analysts on the 46VC Hurricane Venture Fund, a multi-million-dollar student/alumni fund.

items.title: Chair, Macroeconomic Committee — TU Student Investment Fund ($8M)
bullets: Implemented an ETF strategy and decision process still in use today; made buy/sell calls on macro trends and led analysts to execute.

items.title: Master of Business Administration — Finance & Entrepreneurship
B.S. Exercise Science / Pre-Med
items.org: The University of Tulsa  —  Division I Cross Country & Track and Field

sections.heading: Skills & Strengths
items: Industrial / real-economy domain fluency (energy, manufacturing, logistics)
Deal sourcing, screening & diligence at a $100M+ fund
Commercial + technical diligence; engaging engineers, founders & operators credibly
Thesis development from primary research; market sizing; investment memos
Financial, unit-economics & returns modeling; SBIR/STTR non-dilutive funding
Frontier-AI fluency; building production multi-agent / agentic systems
